jokes.text: Tickle tickle tickle! Hey Bret, how come you don't tickle me anymore?
jokes.setup: Two grown men tickling each other, then one asks why the other doesn't tickle him anymore
jokes.punchline: The inherently awkward romantic undertones of the question
jokes.explanation: Establishes the show's signature awkward romantic subtext between the male leads. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Tickle tickle tickle! Hey Bret, how come you don't tickle me anymore?

jokes.text: I'm not even ticklish. / Aren't you? / No, I'm faking it. / Really? / Yeah she's faking it as well, I think.
jokes.setup: Jemaine reveals he's been faking being ticklish
jokes.punchline: His observation that the girl is probably faking it too - sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning about faking sexual pleasure. Good economy and misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I'm not even ticklish.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Aren't you?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, I'm faking it.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah she's faking it as well, I think.

jokes.text: You were robots. / We were just dressed up as robots. / Yeah, but you were robots.
jokes.setup: Sally insists they were robots in their music video
jokes.punchline: Her inability to understand the difference between acting and being
jokes.explanation: Establishes Sally's character as not very bright in an endearing way.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: You were robots.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: We were just dressed up as robots.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you were robots.

jokes.text: So, you got a boyfriend at the moment? / Me? No. You? / Me? I don't have a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Jemaine asks if Sally has a boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Sally's confused response asking if he has a boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Simple miscommunication humor, functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: So, you got a boyfriend at the moment?

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Me? No. You?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Me? I don't have a boyfriend.

jokes.text: You can't tame the J-Dog. / What's the J-Dog? / Well, that's me, I'm the J-Dog.
jokes.setup: Jemaine refers to himself as 'the J-Dog'
jokes.punchline: Sally's confusion and his awkward explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jemaine character moment - trying to be cool but being awkward instead.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You can't tame the J-Dog.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: What's the J-Dog?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, that's me, I'm the J-Dog.

jokes.text: Well, it's street language. You know, you just take the first letter of your name and you put 'Dog' on the end of it and all the other dogs sort of respect you.
jokes.setup: Sally asks where 'J-Dog' comes from
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's completely made-up explanation of 'street language'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Jemaine's tendency to fabricate knowledge. Well-structured absurdist logic.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, it's street language. You know, you just take the first letter of your name and you put 'Dog' on the end of it and all the other dogs sort of respect you.

jokes.text: I thought it was from a kid's book or something.
jokes.setup: Jemaine's elaborate 'street language' explanation
jokes.punchline: Sally's casual dismissal suggesting it sounds childish
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jemaine's pretension with perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: I thought it was from a kid's book or something.

jokes.text: No, the thing about the J-Dog is you can't put a leash on the J-Dog.
jokes.setup: Continuing the J-Dog persona
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous metaphor of putting a leash on himself
jokes.explanation: Extends the J-Dog bit effectively with absurd dog metaphors.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, the thing about the J-Dog is you can't put a leash on the J-Dog.

jokes.text: Because it's Wednesday / And Wednesday night is the night that we usually make love
jokes.setup: Romantic song about making love
jokes.punchline: The unromantic scheduling of intimacy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic song expectations with mundane scheduling reality.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Because it's Wednesday / And Wednesday night is the night that we usually make love

jokes.text: Tuesday night is the night that we usually go to your mother's place / And I teach her how to use the video machine again
jokes.setup: Continuing the weekly schedule
jokes.punchline: The repetitive, mundane nature of helping with technology
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about relationship routines and technology gaps.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Tuesday night is the night that we usually go to your mother's place / And I teach her how to use the video machine again

jokes.text: You're not too tired from your afterwork social netball team practice
jokes.setup: Listing conditions for lovemaking
jokes.punchline: The very specific and unglamorous activity
jokes.explanation: Continues the mundane reality theme with specific detail.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You're not too tired from your afterwork social netball team practice

jokes.text: You turn to me and say something sexy like / 'I might go to bed, I have to work in the morning'
jokes.setup: Promising something sexy she'll say
jokes.punchline: The completely unsexy, practical statement about work
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and perfect observation about unromantic relationship reality.

dialogue.line: You turn to me and say something sexy like / 'I might go to bed, I have to work in the morning'

jokes.text: I know what you're trying to say, baby / You're trying to say 'Ah, yeah / It's business time'
jokes.setup: Interpreting her mundane statement
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous phrase 'business time' for sex
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that became a cultural reference. Perfect absurdist logic and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I know what you're trying to say, baby / You're trying to say 'Ah, yeah / It's business time'

jokes.text: The next thing you know we're in the bathroom brushing our teeth / That's all part of it, that's foreplay
jokes.setup: Describing romantic progression
jokes.punchline: Calling tooth brushing 'foreplay'
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about mundane relationship rituals reframed as romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: The next thing you know we're in the bathroom brushing our teeth / That's all part of it, that's foreplay

jokes.text: Then you go sort out the recycling / Which isn't part of the foreplay / But it's still very important
jokes.setup: Continuing the foreplay description
jokes.punchline: The clarification that recycling isn't foreplay but is still important
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery about environmental responsibility vs. romance.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Then you go sort out the recycling / Which isn't part of the foreplay / But it's still very important

jokes.text: You're wearing that same old ugly baggy T-shirt / With the stain on it that you got / From that team-building exercise / You did for your old work several years ago / Team Building Exercise '99
jokes.setup: Describing romantic attire
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and unsexy details culminating in 'Team Building Exercise '99'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of mundane detail ending with the perfectly specific year.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You're wearing that same old ugly baggy T-shirt / With the stain on it that you got / From that team-building exercise / You did for your old work several years ago / Team Building Exercise '99

jokes.text: I take off my clothes, but I trip over my jeans / Because I'm still wearing my shoes / But it's okay because I turn it all into a sexy dance
jokes.setup: Describing undressing
jokes.punchline: Tripping but claiming it's a sexy dance
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy and character work showing Jemaine's self-deception.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I take off my clothes, but I trip over my jeans / Because I'm still wearing my shoes / But it's okay because I turn it all into a sexy dance

jokes.text: The next thing you know, I'm wearing absolutely nothing / Except for my socks / And you know when I'm down to just my socks / What time it is / It's business time
jokes.setup: Building to the climactic moment
jokes.punchline: The callback to 'business time' triggered by wearing only socks
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that creates a perfect absurdist image of socks as the signal for 'business time'.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: The next thing you know, I'm wearing absolutely nothing / Except for my socks / And you know when I'm down to just my socks / What time it is / It's business time

jokes.text: That's why they're called business socks, ooh
jokes.setup: The socks being the signal for business time
jokes.punchline: The made-up term 'business socks'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy creating an instant classic phrase with absurdist logic.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: That's why they're called business socks, ooh

jokes.text: Making love for two / I'm making love for two minutes / When it's with me, you only need two minutes, girl / Because I'm so intense
jokes.setup: Building up his prowess
jokes.punchline: Revealing it only lasts two minutes but claiming it's due to intensity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing delusional self-confidence about obvious inadequacy.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Making love for two / I'm making love for two minutes / When it's with me, you only need two minutes, girl / Because I'm so intense

jokes.text: Two minutes in heaven is better than one minute in heaven
jokes.setup: Trying to justify the two minutes
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous logic that two minutes is better than one
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic showing Jemaine's attempt to rationalize inadequacy.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Two minutes in heaven is better than one minute in heaven

jokes.text: You turn to me and say something sexy like / 'Is that it?!' / I know what you're trying to say, girl / You're trying to say 'Ah, yeah, that's it'
jokes.setup: Another 'sexy' thing she says
jokes.punchline: Completely misinterpreting her obvious disappointment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing delusional interpretation of obvious criticism.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You turn to me and say something sexy like / 'Is that it?!' / I know what you're trying to say, girl / You're trying to say 'Ah, yeah, that's it'

jokes.text: Then you tell me you want some more / Well, uh, I'm not surprised / But I am quite sleepy
jokes.setup: She wants more
jokes.punchline: His arrogant response followed by the excuse about being sleepy
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing selfishness disguised as confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Then you tell me you want some more / Well, uh, I'm not surprised / But I am quite sleepy

jokes.text: Business hours are over / Baby

jokes.text: Well, here's the doghouse.

jokes.text: I can't believe you're still living with Bret. / Jemaine, you gotta get your own place. / You need your own space.
jokes.setup: Sally's shock at the living situation
jokes.punchline: The implication that two grown men living together is preventing romance
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main plot but isn't particularly funny on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: I can't believe you're still living with Bret. / Jemaine, you gotta get your own place. / You need your own space.

jokes.text: Bret, I'm moving out. / I've decided I need my own space. / It's time to take life a little more seriously, you know? / It's time for me to look each day in the eye and say / 'Hey, Jemaine you're... you're doing this for you.'
jokes.setup: Jemaine making a serious life announcement
jokes.punchline: The awkward, pseudo-motivational speech that trails off
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Jemaine's attempt at self-help speak.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Bret, I'm moving out. I've decided I need my own space. It's time to take life a little more seriously, you know? It's time for me to look each day in the eye and say 'Hey, Jemaine you're... you're doing this for you.'

jokes.text: What's that, man? / Oh didn't you hear that? / What did you say? I wasn't listening.
jokes.setup: Jemaine's big announcement
jokes.punchline: Bret completely missed it because he wasn't listening
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the dramatic moment with typical Bret obliviousness.

dialogue.line: Oh didn't you hear that?

jokes.text: Well she can't go. It's her birthday, the 14th. / I think, maybe. / Yeah, no, that's right.

jokes.punchline: His uncertainty about when her birthday actually is
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's desperation but the execution is weak.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Well she can't go. It's her birthday, the 14th.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I think, maybe.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Yeah, no, that's right.

jokes.text: There's only going to be five people if you don't come.
jokes.setup: Jemaine trying to get Bret to DJ his party
jokes.punchline: Revealing how few people are actually coming to his party
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of how unpopular/unsuccessful Jemaine's party is.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: There's only going to be five people if you don't come.

jokes.text: I'll find my way to you, Sally
jokes.setup: Romantic serenade
jokes.punchline: The awkward direct address in the middle of a song
jokes.explanation: Mildly awkward romantic song moment but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I'll find my way to you, Sally

jokes.text: That's beautiful. / That's a sure thing, bro. / What's a Chinese junk? / Oh, it's a kind of ship.
jokes.setup: Murray praising the song as a 'sure thing'
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's confusion about his own lyric
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's tendency to use words he doesn't understand.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: That's beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: That's a sure thing, bro.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: What's a Chinese junk?
Oh, it's a kind of ship.

jokes.text: Oh yeah, sure. Women love that kind of sensitive nautical shit. / Really? / Yeah. / Haven't you ever seen 'Watership Down'? / No. / Well, me neither, but women love that film.
jokes.setup: Murray giving advice about what women love
jokes.punchline: His confession that he hasn't seen the movie he's referencing
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Murray's false expertise and the absurd movie reference.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, sure. Women love that kind of sensitive nautical shit.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Haven't you ever seen 'Watership Down'?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, me neither, but women love that film.

jokes.text: I mean, that reminds me of when I saw a puppy being born. / I mean, just to see its little head / just coming out of that dog's pussy. / What an incredible moment, man... beautiful.
jokes.setup: Murray making a heartfelt comparison to beauty
jokes.punchline: The extremely graphic and inappropriate birth description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray character moment - inappropriate oversharing disguised as sensitivity.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I mean, that reminds me of when I saw a puppy being born. / I mean, just to see its little head / just coming out of that dog's pussy. / What an incredible moment, man... beautiful.

jokes.text: Do you think that looks like Sally's eyes? / I don't know, Bret. I don't think about her as much as you do.
jokes.setup: Bret obsessing over Sally's eye color in his painting
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's sarcastic response about not thinking about her as much
jokes.explanation: Good ironic response since Jemaine is equally obsessed with Sally.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Do you think that looks like Sally's eyes?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I don't know, Bret. I don't think about her as much as you do.

jokes.text: Hey, get off my pillow. You're on my pillow. / Oh, sorry.
jokes.setup: Party guests in his tiny apartment
jokes.punchline: The reality that his bed is so prominent that guests sit on his pillow
jokes.explanation: Shows the reality of his living situation but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Hey, get off my pillow. You're on my pillow.

dialogue.speaker: Guest
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry.

jokes.text: Planet Jemaine supernova'd. / Yeah, there's nothing left of it apparently. / Just a huge gaseous cloud and the beginnings of a black hole. / When did this happen? / Uh, about four million years ago.
jokes.setup: Murray delivering bad news about Jemaine's star investment
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that it happened four million years ago
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist callback with perfect deadpan delivery about cosmic timescales vs. investment losses.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Planet Jemaine supernova'd. Yeah, there's nothing left of it apparently. Just a huge gaseous cloud and the beginnings of a black hole.
When did this happen?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Uh, about four million years ago.

jokes.text: Planet Jemaine supernova'd. Yeah, there's nothing left of it apparently. Just a huge gaseous cloud and the beginnings of a black hole. When did this happen? Uh, about four million years ago.
jokes.setup: Bad news about investment
jokes.punchline: Cosmic destruction delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of fake investment scam with cosmic absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Uh, about four million years ago.

jokes.text: Well, that was a waste of 50 bucks. / Thanks, Murray.
jokes.setup: Learning his planet was destroyed millions of years ago
jokes.punchline: His understated reaction focusing on the monetary loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to cosmic disaster, showing Jemaine's practical priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, that was a waste of 50 bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Thanks, Murray.

jokes.text: The party's over, everyone. / What? / The party's over. / What are you doing, Jemaine? / Greg's about to do his party trick. / This is the one that got him in 'The East Village News.' / No, the party's over.
jokes.setup: Jemaine suddenly ending his own party
jokes.punchline: The guests' confusion and mention of Greg's newsworthy party trick being canceled
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's impulsiveness and the guests' disappointment but not exceptional.

What are you doing, Jemaine?
Greg's about to do his party trick.
This is the one that got him in 'The East Village News.'

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, the party's over.

jokes.text: Sorry your party was canceled. / Yeah, me too. Nice haircut, Bret.
jokes.setup: Sally's sympathy about the canceled party
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's passive-aggressive comment about Bret's haircut
jokes.explanation: Shows the tension between the friends but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Sorry your party was canceled.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, me too. Nice haircut, Bret.

jokes.text: It's a butterfly. / I sat on it in the subway. / Oh, you shouldn't have. / But I also got you some glue. / And that's... that's multipurpose. / You can use... use that around the house.
jokes.setup: Jemaine giving Sally a broken butterfly gift
jokes.punchline: His attempt to make the glue seem like a thoughtful addition rather than necessity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jemaine trying to salvage a romantic disaster.

jokes.punchline: Bret's admission that he used himself as a model for the wolf
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic showing Bret's artistic limitations and weird problem-solving.

jokes.text: I got engaged. Mark and I are getting married. / Who's Mark? / Mark, he was my boyfriend and then we broke up and now he's my fiancé.
jokes.setup: Sally announcing her engagement
jokes.punchline: Her circular explanation of Mark's relationship progression
jokes.explanation: Shows Sally's simple way of explaining relationships but not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: I got engaged. Mark and I are getting married.
Who's Mark?

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Mark, he was my boyfriend and then we broke up and now he's my fiancé.

jokes.text: He bought me a BMW.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she got engaged
jokes.punchline: The materialistic reason for the engagement
jokes.explanation: Shows Sally's shallow values with good economy but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: He bought me a BMW.

jokes.text: He's little, he's like a pixie. Come on. / Brettie, aye, ohh yes! / Isn't he strong?
jokes.setup: Mark showing off by lifting Bret
jokes.punchline: The emasculating comparison to a pixie and the easy lift
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing the contrast between Mark and the guys but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Mark
dialogue.line: Brettie, aye, ohh yes!

dialogue.line: Isn't he strong?

jokes.text: But I don't just love and need you / I love and need and want you too / I don't just love and need you / I love and need and want you too too / Yeah, well, I love, need and want you too too too / There's too many of those / It's just ridiculous
jokes.setup: Competing to express love with increasing intensity
jokes.punchline: The meta-commentary about having too many 'too's
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that becomes self-aware, showing competitive absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: But I don't just love and need you / I love and need and want you too / I don't just love and need you / I love and need and want you too too

jokes.text: But maybe Mark will be involved / In an accident and you'll get / A life insurance payment of half a mil / It's not about the money / But it could set us up financially
jokes.setup: Romantic song about winning Sally back
jokes.punchline: The dark fantasy about Mark's death for financial gain while claiming it's not about money
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and twisted romantic fantasy with perfect character integration.

dialogue.line: But maybe Mark will be involved / In an accident and you'll get / A life insurance payment of half a mil / It's not about the money / But it could set us up financially

jokes.setup: Bret delivering a message from Coco
jokes.punchline: The casual, secondhand breakup delivery and Jemaine's confused reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the awkwardness of secondhand breakups.

dialogue.line: Oh, Coco told me to tell you you're dumped.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Oh... oh, sorry, man.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine

jokes.text: She said to say that you've been ignoring her / and that you're clearly still in love with Sally, / which I agree with, actually... / That's not true. / She said you'd say that and she said to say / it is true and you know it.
jokes.setup: Bret relaying Coco's message
jokes.punchline: The pre-anticipated responses and Bret's editorial comments
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how Coco anticipated all his responses and Bret's inability to stay neutral.
